findings.description: pytest-bdd v8.x registers steps as fixtures in the calling module's namespace; steps in separate modules are not discovered unless modules are registered via pytest_plugins in conftest.py
SKILL.md obs-stack still showed Prometheus v2.55.1 after compose.yaml was updated to v3.5.4 — version drift detected
then_no_latest_tag step had broken logic: image.endswith(image.split('/')[-1]) always returns True for any tagged image, causing false positive for otel-collector
Tempo distroless image (v2.10+) lacks wget/curl/sh, healthcheck cannot be defined — compose.yaml had documented this but healthcheck test didn't exempt tempo
parse library treats backslash as escape character in Gherkin step parameters, mangling regex patterns with \s, \[, \]
ai:suggestion_acceptance_rate:ratio placeholder rule (expr: 0) was missing or vector(0) guard — test for all recording rules flagged it
Docker and git CLIs are not available in the python:3.12-slim test container — compose config and git tag tests skip gracefully

decision: Fixed pytest-bdd step discovery by switching from conftest imports to pytest_plugins registration; fixed 6 test logic bugs (no_latest_tag, tempo healthcheck, FUNDING.yml regex, SKILL.md version, ai-rules vector(0), orphaned feature steps); added graceful skip for Docker/git-dependent tests in container environments; all 67 Gherkin scenarios now pass (64 pass + 3 skip)
blockers: Docker and git unavailable inside python:3.12-slim container — tests skip gracefully but won't validate compose config or git tags in CI unless tests are run with Docker CLI and git installed
